---
title: Accessibilities
editor:
  render-on-save: true
---

Accessibility metrics quantify how easily different land uses (such as shops, parks, or restaurants) can be reached from each point in the street network. These metrics are fundamental to understanding the distribution and proximity of urban amenities.

The following examples use the [`layers.compute_accessibilities`](https://cityseer.benchmarkurbanism.com/metrics/layers#compute-accessibilities) method. The same network pre-processing steps can be followed as shown for the network preparation or centrality examples. A dataset identifying the locations of different land uses is then needed to compute accessibilities over the network.

This method requires:

- A `geopandas` `GeoDataFrame` containing landuse data;
- A `landuse_column_label` identifying the column with the landuse labels;
- The target `accessibility_keys` indicating which landuse types to compute accessibilities for.

The output `GeoDataFrame` will contain new columns for each landuse in three forms:

- A **count** of reachable instances of a given landuse within the specified distance thresholds;
- A **distance-weighted count** of reachable locations, where nearer locations contribute more;
- The **distance to the nearest** instance of a given landuse.

The last example computes mixed land-uses using the [`layers.compute_mixed_uses`](https://cityseer.benchmarkurbanism.com/metrics/layers#compute-mixed-uses) method, which measures the diversity of land uses reachable from each node.

::: {.callout-tip}
These examples assume you have a prepared network. See [Network Preparation](../networks/index.qmd) for how to create one. For background on `geopandas`, see the [GeoPandas chapter](../../class/4_geopandas.qmd) of the Python 101 section.
:::

## Landuse accessibility from geopandas data

Calculate landuse accessibilities from a `geopandas` `GeoDataFrame`.

[Notebook](gpd_accessibility.ipynb)

## Landuse accessibility from OSM data

Calculate landuse accessibilities from OpenStreetMap.

[Notebook](osm_accessibility.ipynb)

## Parks accessibility from OSM data

Calculate park accessibilities from OSM Polygon data.

[Notebook](osm_accessibility_polys.ipynb)

## Calculating mixed-uses

Calculate mixed land-uses.

[Notebook](gpd_mixed_uses.ipynb)

## Adding GTFS transport data

> GTFS transport data support is still experimental.

Add GTFS transportation data to the network.

[Notebook](accessibility_metro.ipynb)
